revenue_recognition.policy: Revenue primarily from franchise contracts with third-party hotel owners. Primary performance obligation is IP license (brand, reservations, property management systems) — right to access IP, recognized as series of distinct services over time. Variable consideration (royalty fees) allocated to distinct services using prescribed variable consideration allocation guidance. Fixed consideration allocated based on estimated standalone selling prices. Revenue recognized using time as measure of progress, in period earned per contract terms. Sales-based royalty fees recognized as hotel room sales occur.
revenue_recognition.franchise_royalty_fees: Percentage of hotel monthly gross room revenue (and in some cases food/beverage and other revenues). Billed and collected monthly. Consideration paid to incentivize hotel owners amortized as reduction of franchise royalty fees over contract life.
revenue_recognition.franchise_sales_coo_fees: Fixed, collected upfront, recognized over term of franchise contract. Not considered significant financing component.
revenue_recognition.termination_fees: Recognized in period payment received (no further service, uncertainty of collection).

accounts_receivable_and_financing.policy: Accounts receivable = amounts due from franchise hotel owners. Financing receivables = loans to certain franchise hotel owners. Allowance for credit losses based on historical collection activity, nature of instrument, current/forecasted business conditions.

franchise_contracts.policy: Capitalize consideration paid to incentivize hotel owners as contract acquisition costs. Together with other incremental costs to obtain contracts, recorded as 'franchise contracts, net' on balance sheet. Amortized straight-line over estimated useful lives (contract term, including extension periods at franchisor's sole option), generally 10–20 years. Amortization begins on hotel opening date or contract execution date, whichever is later.
franchise_contracts.impairment_policy: Evaluate carrying value for indicators of impairment. If indicators exist, compare expected undiscounted future cash flows to net carrying value. If not recoverable and exceeds fair value, recognize impairment loss. Allocate pro rata based on relative carrying values.

franchise_deposits.policy: Application/initiation fees collected at time of application. Recorded as current liability until non-refundable, then reclassified to deferred revenues. If not approved, recorded as other current liability until refunded (less processing fees).

deferred_revenues.policy: Non-refundable advance consideration from hotel owners reclassified from franchise deposits. Recognized over term of related contract. Presented as current and non-current deferred revenues.

fair_value_measurements.policy: Three-level valuation hierarchy (Level 1: quoted prices; Level 2: observable inputs; Level 3: unobservable inputs). Fair values of financial assets and liabilities estimated to equal carrying values as of Dec 31, 2024 and 2023.

acquisitions.policy: Assess whether transaction is business combination or asset acquisition. Business combination when assets include inputs + substantive processes that contribute to ability to create outputs. Otherwise, asset acquisition — allocate cost including direct/incremental transaction costs to assets/liabilities based on relative fair values. No goodwill in asset acquisition.
acquisitions.graduate_acquisition: May 2024, acquired Graduate franchise contracts as part of larger Hilton acquisition of Graduate brand. Accounted for as asset acquisition. Recorded ~$85M franchise contract intangible assets. Amortized over estimated useful life of 15 years.

income_taxes.policy: No provision for US income taxes — entity is disregarded for US income tax purposes, all items of taxable income included in computation of Hilton. Foreign franchise fee taxes presented as foreign tax expense. Franchisee responsible for withholding/remittance of foreign taxes.

lease_accounting.note: No lease disclosures — entity is a franchise-only licensing entity with no significant real property.

note_7_commitments_contingencies.description: Hilton's debt obligations (maturing 2025–2033) are unconditionally and irrevocably guaranteed by certain Hilton wholly owned material domestic subsidiaries including the franchisor. All assets and franchise contracts pledged as collateral for the term of the debt agreements. No guarantee liability recorded due to parent-subsidiary relationship.
note_7_commitments_contingencies.litigation: From time to time involved in various claims and lawsuits. Expects ultimate resolution will not have material adverse effect.

note_8_related_party_transactions.description: Intercompany balances with Hilton affiliates result from centralized cash management system not controlled by the franchisor. Franchise deposits collected by Hilton affiliates and deposited in lockbox to which franchisor has no access. Other receivables settled through periodic distributions of retained earnings to parent.
note_8_related_party_transactions.graduate_acquisition_note: Payment for Graduate franchise intangibles made by affiliate; short-term intercompany note recorded ($85.3M), settled February 2024 with accrued interest.
note_8_related_party_transactions.operator_agreement: Has operator agreement with Hilton affiliate entitling affiliate to reasonable fee. No compensation paid in 2024, 2023, or 2022.

note_9_subsequent_events.description: Evaluated subsequent events through March 18, 2024 [sic — likely March 18, 2025]. No material subsequent events disclosed.
note_9_subsequent_events.date_anomaly: The date 'March 18, 2024' appears to be a typographical error. Financial statements are for year ended December 31, 2024, with issuance date of March 30, 2025.

advertising_fund_accounting.note: Monthly Program Fee (4% of GRR) funds various programs. No separate fund entity. No audited financial statement of program fee expenditures provided.
